data.body:
Added two NEW labeled false-fires to the eval seed (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md) from the e6d9 (workflow-engine-v2) plan-review DRY RUN:

- FP6 [T2/E3] cross-provider 'hard requirement' flagged unvalidated because completion only proven on Anthropic — FALSE FIRE: provider-agnosticism is the adopted library's (Pydantic AI) documented CONTRACT; re-proving it = testing code that isn't ours. New taxonomy mode 10 'library-guaranteed-capability'. Verifier missed it (Pass-2 no_existing_mitigation=no when it should be yes).
- FP7 [A1/E4/G1G2] NIH/'rebuild vs extend' flagged because a near-complete v1 workflow pkg exists — FALSE FIRE: the v1 pkg is a POC/experimental-validation REFERENCE, not production; rebuilding for production is the intended lifecycle (we encourage brainstorming probes). New taxonomy mode 11 'rebuild-of-designated-experiment-reference'. (A1 was already dropped conf .29 this run, but for the wrong reason — needs a reliable carve-out.)

CRUCIAL PRECISION captured in the seed: FP6 must NOT over-suppress the SEPARATE legitimate flag from the same run (T3/T1: does Pydantic AI NativeOutput work for Anthropic specifically — a maturing FEATURE, not a contract). A live experiment resolved that one (NativeOutput sends Anthropic output_config, no tools; works in pydantic_ai 1.107.0). Rule: library-CONTRACT -> drop; library-FEATURE-MATURITY -> keep.

Proposed verifier/criterion tuning (smallest blast radius first) is written into the seed file: a Pass-2 system-prompt rule for mode 10 + T2/T3 anti-FP line; an A1/E4/G1G2 anti-FP clause for mode 11 with a coach-not-assert fallback when production-vs-reference is ambiguous from path. Both gated by the sycophancy-axis recall guardrail.
